title: Super Group Dealerships, a division of Super Group Trading (Pty) Ltd And The Business of Chery Omoda & Jaecoo Mbombela franchise within Stucky Motors Multi-Franchise (Pty) Ltd
text:
COMPETITION TRIBUNAL
REPUBLIC OF SOUTH AFRICA
Case No.: LM188Feb26
In the matter between:
Super Group Dealerships, a division of Super Group Primary Acquiring Firm
Trading Proprietary Limited
And
The Business of Chery Omoda & Jaecoo Mbombela Primary Target Firm
franchise within Stucky Motors Multi-Franchise
Proprietary Limited
Panel: AW Wessels (Presiding Member)
T Vilakazi (Tribunal Member)
A Ndoni (Tribunal Member)
Heard on: 08 April 2026
Decided on: 08 April 2026
ORDER
Further to the recommendation of the Competition Commission in terms of section
14A(1)(b) of the Competition Act, 1998 (“the Act”) the Competition Tribunal orders that–
1. the merger between the abovementioned parties be approved in terms of section
16(2)(a) of the Act; and
2. a Merger Clearance Certificate be issued in terms of Competition Tribunal Rule
35(5)(a).
08 April 2026
Presiding Member Date
Mr Andreas Wessels
Concurring: Prof. Thando Vilakazi and Ms Andiswa Ndoni.

Merger Clearance Certificate
Date : 08 April 2026
To : Super Group Dealerships
Case Number: LM188Feb26
Notice CT 10
Super Group Dealerships, a division of Super Group Trading
Proprietary Limited And The Business of Cherry Omoda Jaecoo
About this Notice
in Mbombela operated by Stucky Motors Multi-Franchise (Pty) Ltd

terms of section 16 of
You applied to the Competition Commission on 12 February 2026
the Competition Act.
for merger approval in accordance with Chapter 3 of the
You may appeal Competition Act.

Appeal Court within 20 Your merger was referred to the Competition Tribunal in terms of

After reviewing all relevant information, and the recommendation
or decision of the Competition Commission, the Competition
Tribunal approves the merger in terms of section 16(2) of the Act,
for the reasons set out in the Reasons for Decision.
This approval is subject to:
x no conditions
the conditions listed on the attached sheet.
The Competition Tribunal has the authority in terms of section 16(3)
of the Competition Act to revoke this approval if
a) it was granted on the basis of incorrect information for which
a party to the merger was responsible.

b) the approval was obtained by deceit.
the Tribunal
